cd-enters-folders.title: `cd` changes the current folder
cd-enters-folders.bodyMarkdown:
`cd` moves your terminal into another folder. After you move, run `pwd` so you know where you landed.

```bash
cd city-tour
pwd
```

When a path is long, build it slowly and check often.

dot-dot-parent.title: `..` means the parent folder
dot-dot-parent.bodyMarkdown:
Use `cd ..` to go up one folder. It is useful when you move into a folder, create something, then need to return.

```bash
cd station
cd ..
```

That small move prevents many path mistakes.

home-shortcut.title: Relative paths start from where you are
home-shortcut.bodyMarkdown:
A relative path is read from your current folder. If you are already inside `city-tour`, then `station/platform` points deeper inside that folder.

Use `pwd` when a command does not affect the file you expected.
